title: millettia ferruginea subsp. ferruginea
intro: Millettia ferruginea subsp. ferruginea, also known as the African ironplow tree, is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the root bark of Millettia ferruginea subsp. ferruginea is often used in traditional medicine, typically in the form of decoctions or infusions.
